title: Moroccan Tofu Mesmen
description: A flavorful and spicy Moroccan-inspired dish featuring crispy tofu and served with a twist on traditional mesmen.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium-high heat.
Cut the tofu into small cubes and place on a plate.
In a shallow dish, mix together flour, salt, cumin, and paprika.
Dredge the tofu cubes in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the preheated pan and cook the tofu until golden brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat the remaining olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chili flakes and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Warm the mesmen sheets according to package instructions.
To assemble the dish, place a sheet of mesmen on a plate, followed by a few pieces of the cooked tofu, and top with the spicy onion and garlic mixture.
